title: invAIriant PR audit — persistent-mesh-transport (genuine-TLS SNI derivation)
scope: The shell renderer for an own-certificate genuine-TLS transport family (transport/render_params.sh, render_server.sh) and its tls_sni derivation. Illustrative case, synthesized from a real, fixed defect class; no project is named.

lens_scores.verdict: the 'own-cert SNI ≠ cover SNI' invariant is not enforced; correctness depends on an incidental wildcard SAN and the guard's fail-closed docstring is false (PMT-002)
on a no-wildcard-SAN node the own-cert family emits a cert/SNI active-probe tell + correlation channel (PMT-001)
the SNI decision is split across two shell files with a silent fallback and no typed owner (PMT-003)
indistinguishability regresses for the affected node class (PMT-001)

findings.claim: The genuine-TLS SNI fallback makes an own-cert family present the node's own certificate under the cover domain's SNI on nodes without a wildcard cert — a cert/SNI active-probe tell and a cross-family correlation channel.

evidence.hunk: -[ -n "$tls_domain" ] || tls_domain="$cover"   # fallback to the cover-domain SNI

evidence.description: on a self-signed cert (CN=cover domain, no wildcard SAN) the wildcard grep is empty, so tls_domain becomes $cover

evidence.doc: transport/render_params.sh (docstring: 'a misconfigured node fails closed rather than shipping a cert/SNI tell')
evidence.description: the SNI guard rejects only an EMPTY tls_sni; the non-empty cover fallback passes, so the fail-closed claim is false and the tell ships

findings.risk: The own-cert genuine-TLS family serves the node's own certificate under the cover domain's SNI: a cert/SNI mismatch detectable by active probing, and a channel correlating the own-cert family with the camouflaged family by shared SNI. Directly hits traffic indistinguishability and anonymity.
findings.recommendation: Drop the cover fallback; leave tls_sni empty when no own-cert domain is derivable so the guard genuinely fail-closes; additionally reject tls_sni == cover_sni. (This is exactly the fix in diff.patch.)

verification.method: read the fallback line and the SNI guard; traced the self-signed-cert path; confirmed the guard rejects only empty
verification.notes: escalates to S0 on affected nodes; held at S1 because live nodes use a real wildcard cert (see rejected-hypotheses)

findings.claim: The invariant that an own-cert family's SNI must never equal the cover SNI is not enforced; correctness depends on whether a wildcard SAN incidentally exists, and the guard's stated fail-closed property is false.

evidence.description: the SNI guard is `[ -n "$_tls_sni" ] || die` — it rejects an empty value only, not tls_sni == cover_sni
the documented invariant is not the one the guard checks

findings.risk: A security property that holds 'because of how it is called' (whether a wildcard cert happens to exist) is not a design property; the false docstring hides the gap from the next reader.
findings.recommendation: Enforce the invariant explicitly (reject tls_sni == cover_sni for enabled own-cert families) and make the docstring describe what the guard actually checks.

findings.claim: The SNI-derivation decision is spread across two shell files with a silent fallback and no single typed owner.

evidence.description: derives tls_sni via cert-SAN parsing with an inline fallback
re-checks the same decision in a separate guard; the two must agree by hand

findings.risk: A security-critical decision with no typed contract drifts: the two sites can disagree, and there is no single owner of the SNI rule.
findings.recommendation: Move SNI derivation and validation behind a single typed contract that both the renderer and the guard consume.

hypotheses.text: All production nodes are exposed by this tell.
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Refuted — live nodes use a real wildcard cert, so the wildcard-SAN branch derives tls_sni and the cover fallback is never taken. Only nodes on a built-in self-signed cert (no wildcard SAN) are affected. This is why PMT-001 is held at S1, not S0.

hypotheses.text: The SNI guard already prevents the tell (it 'fails closed').
hypotheses.rejected_reason: Refuted — the guard rejects only an EMPTY tls_sni; the cover fallback yields a non-empty value that passes. The docstring's fail-closed claim does not match the code.

summary.executive_summary: The renderer's genuine-TLS SNI fallback (|| tls_sni=$cover) makes the own-cert family present its own certificate under the cover domain's SNI on nodes without a wildcard cert — a cert/SNI active-probe tell and a cross-family correlation channel (PMT-001, S1). The invariant is not enforced and the guard's fail-closed docstring is false (PMT-002). A diff-level reviewer approves a documented, working fallback; the lenses do not. Live nodes with real wildcard certs are unaffected, holding PMT-001 at S1. ci-gate blocks the merge until fixed.
summary.weakest_reason: the emitted config is distinguishable under active probing on the affected node class (PMT-001)

required_actions.action: Drop the cover fallback; leave tls_sni empty when undecidable (guard fail-closes); reject tls_sni == cover_sni.
Move SNI derivation/validation behind a single typed contract.
